title.en: Biographies of Yuan Ang and Chao Cuo
title.raw: 《袁盎鼂錯列傳》 Biographies of Yuan Ang and Chao Cuo

sentences.zh: 袁盎者，楚人也，字絲。

translations.literal: Yuan Ang was a man of Chu, with the courtesy name Si.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang was a native of Chu, and his courtesy name was Si.

sentences.zh: 父故為群盜，徙處安陵。

translations.literal: His father had formerly been among a band of robbers, and was relocated to settle in Anling.
translations.idiomatic: His father had once been a bandit and was later resettled in Anling.

sentences.zh: 高后時，盎嘗為呂祿舍人。

translations.literal: During the time of Empress Dowager Gao, Ang once served as a retainer of Lü Lu.
translations.idiomatic: During the reign of Empress Dowager Gao, Ang served as a retainer in the household of Lü Lu.

sentences.zh: 及孝文帝即位，盎兄噲任盎為中郎。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xiaowen ascended the throne, Ang's elder brother Kuai recommended Ang to serve as a Gentleman of the Palace.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wen came to the throne, Ang's elder brother Kuai secured him a position as Gentleman of the Palace.

sentences.zh: 絳侯為丞相，朝罷趨出，意得甚。

translations.literal: When the Marquis of Jiang served as Chancellor, after court sessions he would hasten out with a look of great self-satisfaction.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Jiang served as Chancellor, and whenever court was dismissed, he would stride out looking enormously pleased with himself.

sentences.zh: 上禮之恭，常自送之。

translations.literal: The Emperor treated him with such reverent courtesy that he would personally escort him out.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor showed him such deference that he would personally see him off.

sentences.zh: 袁盎進曰：「陛下以丞相何如人？」

translations.literal: Yuan Ang stepped forward and said: 'Your Majesty, what kind of man do you consider the Chancellor to be?'
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang came forward and asked, 'Your Majesty, what sort of man do you think the Chancellor is?'

sentences.zh: 上曰：「社稷臣。」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'A minister of the altars of state.'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor replied, 'A pillar of the state.'

sentences.zh: 盎曰：「絳侯所謂功臣，非社稷臣，社稷臣主在與在，主亡與亡。

translations.literal: Ang said: 'The Marquis of Jiang is what is called a meritorious minister, not a minister of the altars of state. A minister of the altars of state lives when the sovereign lives and perishes when the sovereign perishes.'
translations.idiomatic: Ang said, 'The Marquis of Jiang is a meritorious minister, not a pillar of the state. A true pillar of the state shares his sovereign's fate—he rises with his lord and falls with him.'

sentences.zh: 方呂后時，諸呂用事，擅相王，劉氏不絕如帶。

translations.literal: During the time of Empress Lü, the Lü clan held power, freely appointing chancellors and kings, and the survival of the Liu clan hung by a thread like a thin ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Lü ruled, her kinsmen seized power, appointing chancellors and kings at will, and the House of Liu survived by only a thread.'

sentences.zh: 是時絳侯為太尉，主兵柄，弗能正。

translations.literal: At that time the Marquis of Jiang held the post of Grand Commandant, commanding military authority, yet he could not set things right.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, the Marquis of Jiang was Grand Commandant with command of the armies, yet he could not set things right.

sentences.zh: 呂后崩，大臣相與共畔諸呂，太尉主兵，適會其成功，所謂功臣，非社稷臣。

translations.literal: When Empress Lü died, the great ministers joined together to overthrow the Lü clan. The Grand Commandant commanded the troops and happened to be present for the achievement of success. This is what is called a meritorious minister, not a minister of the altars of state.
translations.idiomatic: After Empress Lü died, the senior ministers banded together to overthrow the Lü clan. The Grand Commandant led the troops and happened to be there when success was achieved. That makes him a meritorious minister, not a pillar of the state.

sentences.zh: 丞相如有驕主色。

translations.literal: The Chancellor seems to show an air of arrogance toward the sovereign.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor carries himself with an air of arrogance toward Your Majesty.

sentences.zh: 陛下謙讓，臣主失禮，竊為陛下不取也。」

translations.literal: Your Majesty shows humility and deference, yet the proper rites between minister and sovereign are lost. I privately consider this inadvisable for Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty has been humble and yielding, but the proper decorum between sovereign and minister has been lost. I venture to say that this is beneath Your Majesty.'

sentences.zh: 後朝，上益莊，丞相益畏。

translations.literal: At subsequent court sessions, the Emperor became increasingly dignified, and the Chancellor became increasingly fearful.
translations.idiomatic: From then on, the Emperor grew more commanding at court, and the Chancellor became ever more apprehensive.

sentences.zh: 已而絳侯望袁盎曰：「吾與而兄善，今兒廷毀我！」

translations.literal: Soon after, the Marquis of Jiang glared at Yuan Ang resentfully and said: 'I was on good terms with your brother, and now you, boy, slander me at court!'
translations.idiomatic: Before long, the Marquis of Jiang confronted Yuan Ang with a glare and said, 'I was on good terms with your brother, and now you, boy, denounce me at court!'

sentences.zh: 盎遂不謝。

translations.literal: Ang thereupon did not apologize.
translations.idiomatic: Ang offered no apology.

sentences.zh: 及絳侯免相之國，國人上書告以為反，徵系清室，宗室諸公莫敢為言，唯袁盎明絳侯無罪。

translations.literal: When the Marquis of Jiang was dismissed as Chancellor and returned to his fief, someone from his domain submitted a memorial accusing him of plotting rebellion. He was summoned and imprisoned in the special detention cells. None of the members of the imperial clan or senior officials dared speak on his behalf. Only Yuan Ang testified that the Marquis of Jiang was innocent.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Jiang was dismissed from the chancellorship and returned to his fief, someone from his domain submitted a memorial accusing him of rebellion. He was summoned and confined in the imperial prison. Not a single member of the imperial clan or the senior ministers dared to speak in his defense—only Yuan Ang declared the Marquis of Jiang to be innocent.

sentences.zh: 絳侯得釋，盎頗有力。

translations.literal: When the Marquis of Jiang was released, Ang had contributed considerably to the effort.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Jiang was released, it was largely thanks to Ang's efforts.

sentences.zh: 絳侯乃大與盎結交。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Jiang thereupon formed a deep friendship with Ang.
translations.idiomatic: From then on, the Marquis of Jiang became a close friend and ally of Ang.

sentences.zh: 淮南厲王朝，殺辟陽侯，居處驕甚。

translations.literal: The Fierce King of Huainan came to court, killed the Marquis of Biyang, and conducted himself with extreme arrogance.
translations.idiomatic: The Fierce King of Huainan came to court, killed the Marquis of Biyang, and behaved with extreme arrogance.

sentences.zh: 袁盎諫曰：「諸侯大驕必生患，可適削地。」

translations.literal: Yuan Ang remonstrated, saying: 'When feudal lords become too arrogant, trouble is sure to follow. It would be best to punish him by reducing his territory.'
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang remonstrated, 'When feudal lords grow too proud, disaster is sure to follow. You should discipline him by reducing his territory.'

sentences.zh: 上弗用。

translations.literal: The Emperor did not adopt his advice.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor did not heed his advice.

sentences.zh: 淮南王益橫。

translations.literal: The King of Huainan grew ever more unruly.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan became increasingly lawless.

sentences.zh: 及棘蒲侯柴武太子謀反事覺，治，連淮南王，淮南王徵，上因遷之蜀，轞車傳送。

translations.literal: When the conspiracy of the heir of the Marquis of Jipu, Chai Wu, was discovered and investigated, the case implicated the King of Huainan. The King of Huainan was summoned, and the Emperor ordered him exiled to Shu, transported by prisoner's cart through relay stations.
translations.idiomatic: When the plot of the heir of the Marquis of Jipu, Chai Wu, was uncovered and investigated, the trail led to the King of Huainan. The king was summoned, and the Emperor ordered him banished to Shu, to be transported in a prisoner's cart from station to station.

sentences.zh: 袁盎時為中郎將，乃諫曰：「陛下素驕淮南王，弗稍禁，以至此，今又暴摧折之。

translations.literal: Yuan Ang, who was then serving as Commander of the Gentlemen of the Palace, remonstrated: 'Your Majesty has long indulged the King of Huainan and never gradually restrained him, which has brought matters to this point. Now you would suddenly crush him.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang, then serving as Commander of the Gentlemen of the Palace, remonstrated, 'Your Majesty has always indulged the King of Huainan and never reined him in, which is how things came to this pass. Now you would break him all at once.

sentences.zh: 淮南王為人剛，如有遇霧露行道死，陛下竟為以天下之大弗能容，有殺弟之名，柰何？」

translations.literal: The King of Huainan is a man of unyielding temperament. If he should encounter fog and dew on the road and die of illness, Your Majesty would ultimately bear the reputation that, possessing all the vastness of the realm, you could not make room for your own brother, gaining the name of one who killed his brother. What then?'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan is a man of unyielding spirit. If he should fall ill on the road and die from exposure, Your Majesty will be seen as one who, despite ruling all under heaven, could not abide his own brother—and will bear the name of a fratricide. What then?'

sentences.zh: 上弗聽，遂行之。

translations.literal: The Emperor did not listen and proceeded with the order.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor refused to listen and carried out the order.

sentences.zh: 淮南王至雍，病死，聞，上輟食，哭甚哀。

translations.literal: When the King of Huainan reached Yong, he fell ill and died. When the Emperor heard the news, he stopped eating and wept in deep sorrow.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan died of illness on the road at Yong. When the Emperor received the news, he stopped eating and wept bitterly.

sentences.zh: 盎入，頓首請罪。

translations.literal: Ang entered and, bowing his head to the ground, begged forgiveness for his offense.
translations.idiomatic: Ang came before the Emperor, kowtowed, and begged to be punished.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「以不用公言至此。」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'It is because I did not heed your words that things have come to this.'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor said, 'It is because I did not listen to you that things have come to this.'

sentences.zh: 盎曰：「上自寬，此往事，豈可悔哉！

translations.literal: Ang said: 'May Your Majesty take comfort. This is a matter of the past—how can it be undone through regret?
translations.idiomatic: Ang said, 'Your Majesty should put this behind you. What is done is done—regret will not undo it.

sentences.zh: 且陛下有高世之行者三，此不足以毀名。」

translations.literal: Moreover, Your Majesty has three deeds that surpass all others of this age, and this alone is not enough to tarnish your reputation.'
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, Your Majesty has three deeds that surpass those of any age, and this alone is not enough to tarnish your name.'

sentences.zh: 上曰：「吾高世行三者何事？」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'What are these three deeds of mine that surpass the age?'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor asked, 'What are these three surpassing deeds of mine?'

sentences.zh: 盎曰：「陛下居代時，太后嘗病，三年，陛下不交睫，不解衣，湯藥非陛下口所嘗弗進。

translations.literal: Ang said: 'When Your Majesty resided in Dai, the Empress Dowager once fell ill for three years. During that time, Your Majesty never closed your eyes to sleep, never removed your robes, and would not allow any medicine to be administered unless you had first tasted it yourself.
translations.idiomatic: Ang said, 'When Your Majesty was King of Dai, the Empress Dowager fell ill, and for three years you never closed your eyes to rest, never changed your robes, and refused to let any medicine be given to her unless you had tasted it first.

sentences.zh: 夫曾參以布衣猶難之，今陛下親以王者修之，過曾參孝遠矣。

translations.literal: Even Zeng Shen, as a commoner, found such devotion difficult. Now Your Majesty has personally practiced it as a king, surpassing the filial piety of Zeng Shen by far.
translations.idiomatic: Even Zeng Shen, a commoner, found such devotion difficult to sustain. Yet Your Majesty practiced it as a king, far surpassing the filial piety of Zeng Shen.

sentences.zh: 夫諸呂用事，大臣專制，然陛下從代乘六傳馳不測之淵，雖賁育之勇不及陛下。

translations.literal: When the Lü clan held power and the great ministers ruled arbitrarily, Your Majesty rode from Dai by relay carriages and raced into an unfathomable abyss of danger. Even the courage of Ben and Yu could not match Your Majesty's.
translations.idiomatic: When the Lü clan held power and the ministers ruled as they pleased, Your Majesty rode from Dai by relay carriage and plunged headlong into an abyss of danger. Even the legendary warriors Ben and Yu could not match such courage.

sentences.zh: 陛下至代邸，西向讓天子位者再，南面讓天子位者三。

translations.literal: When Your Majesty arrived at the residence of the King of Dai, you twice declined the throne while facing west, and three times declined it while facing south.
translations.idiomatic: When Your Majesty arrived at the Dai residence, you declined the throne twice while facing west, and three more times while facing south.

sentences.zh: 夫許由一讓，而陛下五以天下讓，過許由四矣。

translations.literal: Xu You declined once, yet Your Majesty declined the empire five times—surpassing Xu You by four.
translations.idiomatic: The sage Xu You declined the throne once, but Your Majesty declined the empire five times, surpassing Xu You fourfold.

sentences.zh: 且陛下遷淮南王，欲以苦其志，使改過，有司衛不謹，故病死。」

translations.literal: Moreover, when Your Majesty exiled the King of Huainan, the intention was to discipline his spirit and cause him to mend his ways. It was the negligence of the officials guarding him that led to his death from illness.'
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, when Your Majesty exiled the King of Huainan, the intention was to chasten his spirit and bring him to repentance. It was the negligence of the escorting officials that caused his death.'

sentences.zh: 於是上乃解，曰：「將柰何？」

translations.literal: At this the Emperor's grief was eased, and he said: 'What should be done now?'
translations.idiomatic: At this the Emperor's spirits lifted, and he asked, 'What should be done?'

sentences.zh: 盎曰：「淮南王有三子，唯在陛下耳。」

translations.literal: Ang said: 'The King of Huainan has three sons. It is entirely up to Your Majesty.'
translations.idiomatic: Ang said, 'The King of Huainan had three sons. The matter rests entirely with Your Majesty.'

sentences.zh: 於是文帝立其三子皆為王。

translations.literal: Thereupon Emperor Wen enfeoffed all three of the King's sons as kings.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen then enfeoffed all three of the late king's sons as kings.

sentences.zh: 盎由此名重朝廷。

translations.literal: From this, Ang's reputation gained great weight at court.
translations.idiomatic: From then on, Ang's reputation carried great weight at court.

sentences.zh: 袁盎常引大體慨。

translations.literal: Yuan Ang always drew upon matters of great principle and spoke with impassioned concern.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang always spoke of matters of great principle with impassioned conviction.

sentences.zh: 宦者趙同以數幸，常害袁盎，袁盎患之。

translations.literal: The eunuch Zhao Tong, who enjoyed frequent imperial favor, constantly sought to harm Yuan Ang, and Yuan Ang was troubled by this.
translations.idiomatic: The eunuch Zhao Tong, who had long enjoyed the Emperor's favor, constantly sought to undermine Yuan Ang, and Ang was troubled by this.

sentences.zh: 盎兄子種為常侍騎，持節夾乘，說盎曰：「君與鬬，廷辱之，使其毀不用。」

translations.literal: Ang's nephew Zhong served as an Attendant Cavalry officer and rode alongside the imperial carriage bearing a tally. He advised Ang: 'You should clash with him and humiliate him at court, so that his slanders lose their effect.'
translations.idiomatic: Ang's nephew Zhong served as an Attendant Cavalry officer who rode beside the imperial carriage bearing a tally. He advised Ang, 'Pick a fight with him and humiliate him at court, so that his slanders lose their sting.'

sentences.zh: 孝文帝出，趙同參乘，袁盎伏車前曰：「臣聞天子所與共六尺輿者，皆天下豪英。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xiaowen went out, Zhao Tong rode as a companion in the imperial carriage. Yuan Ang prostrated himself before the carriage and said: 'I have heard that those who share the six-foot carriage with the Son of Heaven should all be the outstanding heroes of the realm.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wen went out, Zhao Tong rode as a companion in the imperial carriage. Yuan Ang threw himself before the carriage and said, 'I have heard that those who share the Son of Heaven's carriage should be the finest heroes of the realm.

sentences.zh: 今漢雖乏人，陛下獨奈何與刀鋸餘人載！」

translations.literal: Although the Han dynasty may lack men, how can Your Majesty alone ride with a remnant of the knife and saw!'
translations.idiomatic: Though the Han may be short of men, how can Your Majesty ride with the leavings of the knife and saw!'

sentences.zh: 於是上笑，下趙同。

translations.literal: At this the Emperor laughed and ordered Zhao Tong to step down.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor laughed and ordered Zhao Tong to get off.

sentences.zh: 趙同泣下車。

translations.literal: Zhao Tong wept as he descended from the carriage.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Tong climbed down from the carriage in tears.

sentences.zh: 文帝從霸陵上，欲西馳下峻阪。

translations.literal: Emperor Wen ascended the heights at Baling and wished to gallop westward down the steep slope.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen ascended the heights at Baling and wanted to gallop down the steep western slope.

sentences.zh: 袁盎騎，并車擥轡。

translations.literal: Yuan Ang rode up alongside the carriage and seized the reins.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang rode up beside the carriage and seized the reins.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「將軍怯邪？」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'Is the General a coward?'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor said, 'Are you afraid, General?'

sentences.zh: 盎曰：「臣聞千金之子坐不垂堂，百金之子不騎衡，聖主不乘危而徼幸。

translations.literal: Ang said: 'I have heard that the son of a house worth a thousand gold pieces does not sit at the edge of the eaves, the son of a house worth a hundred gold pieces does not lean over a railing on horseback, and a sage ruler does not court danger for the sake of a thrill.
translations.idiomatic: Ang replied, 'I have heard that a man whose family is worth a thousand in gold does not sit at the edge of the roof, a man worth a hundred in gold does not lean over a railing on horseback, and a wise sovereign does not court danger for the sake of a thrill.

sentences.zh: 今陛下騁六騑，馳下峻山，如有馬驚車敗，陛下縱自輕，柰高廟、太后何？」

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty drives six steeds at full gallop down a steep mountain. If a horse should bolt and the carriage overturn, even if Your Majesty makes light of your own person, what of the ancestral temple and the Empress Dowager?'
translations.idiomatic: Now Your Majesty drives a team of six at full gallop down a steep mountain. If the horses bolt and the carriage overturns, even if Your Majesty makes light of your own life, what of the ancestral temple and the Empress Dowager?'

sentences.zh: 上乃止。

translations.literal: The Emperor thereupon stopped.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor relented.

sentences.zh: 上幸上林，皇后、慎夫人從。

translations.literal: The Emperor visited the Shanglin Park, accompanied by the Empress and Lady Shen.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor visited the Shanglin Park, accompanied by the Empress and Lady Shen.

sentences.zh: 其在禁中，常同席坐。

translations.literal: When they were in the palace, they customarily sat together on the same mat.
translations.idiomatic: In the palace, the two customarily sat side by side on the same mat.

sentences.zh: 及坐，郎署長布席，袁盎引卻慎夫人坐。

translations.literal: When they were about to sit, the head of the Gentleman's Office laid out the mats, and Yuan Ang pulled back Lady Shen's seat to a separate position.
translations.idiomatic: When the seats were being arranged, the head of the Gentleman's Office laid out the mats, and Yuan Ang drew Lady Shen's seat back to a separate position.

sentences.zh: 慎夫人怒，不肯坐。

translations.literal: Lady Shen was angry and refused to sit.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Shen was furious and refused to sit down.

sentences.zh: 上亦怒，起，入禁中。

translations.literal: The Emperor was also angered. He rose and withdrew into the inner palace.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor was also angered. He stood and withdrew into the inner palace.

sentences.zh: 盎因前說曰：「臣聞尊卑有序則上下和。

translations.literal: Ang thereupon came forward and explained: 'I have heard that when the noble and the base observe their proper order, harmony prevails between those above and below.
translations.idiomatic: Ang then came forward and explained, 'I have heard that when the high and the low observe their proper order, harmony prevails between those above and those below.

sentences.zh: 今陛下既已立后，慎夫人乃妾，妾主豈可與同坐哉！

translations.literal: Now that Your Majesty has already established the Empress, Lady Shen is a concubine. How can a concubine and her mistress sit together as equals!
translations.idiomatic: Now that Your Majesty has installed the Empress, Lady Shen is a concubine. How can a concubine sit as an equal with her mistress!

sentences.zh: 適所以失尊卑矣。

translations.literal: This is precisely what undermines the distinction between the noble and the base.
translations.idiomatic: That is precisely how the distinction of rank is lost.

sentences.zh: 且陛下幸之，即厚賜之。

translations.literal: Moreover, if Your Majesty favors her, then reward her generously.
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty favors her, then shower her with gifts.

sentences.zh: 陛下所以為慎夫人，適所以禍之。

translations.literal: What Your Majesty does for Lady Shen's sake is precisely what will bring disaster upon her.
translations.idiomatic: What Your Majesty does for Lady Shen is precisely what will bring disaster upon her.

sentences.zh: 陛下獨不見『人彘』乎？」

translations.literal: Has Your Majesty not seen what became of the "Human Swine"?'
translations.idiomatic: Has Your Majesty forgotten the fate of the Human Swine?'

sentences.zh: 於是上乃說，召語慎夫人。

translations.literal: At this the Emperor was pleased. He summoned Lady Shen and told her what Ang had said.
translations.idiomatic: At this the Emperor was persuaded. He summoned Lady Shen and explained Ang's reasoning to her.

sentences.zh: 慎夫人賜盎金五十斤。

translations.literal: Lady Shen bestowed upon Ang fifty catties of gold.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Shen presented Ang with fifty catties of gold.

sentences.zh: 然袁盎亦以數直諫，不得久居中，調為隴西都尉。

translations.literal: However, because Yuan Ang had so often spoken bluntly in remonstrance, he could not remain long at the capital and was transferred to serve as Commandant of Longxi.
translations.idiomatic: Yet because Yuan Ang had so often spoken bluntly in remonstrance, he could not remain long at court and was transferred to serve as Commandant of Longxi.

sentences.zh: 仁愛士卒，士卒皆爭為死。

translations.literal: He treated the soldiers with benevolence and love, and the soldiers all vied to die for him.
translations.idiomatic: He treated his soldiers with kindness and generosity, and they all competed for the chance to die for him.

sentences.zh: 遷為齊相。

translations.literal: He was promoted to Chancellor of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: He was then promoted to Chancellor of Qi.

sentences.zh: 徙為吳相，辭行，種謂盎曰：「吳王驕日久，國多姦。

translations.literal: He was transferred to serve as Chancellor of Wu. When he took his leave, Zhong said to Ang: 'The King of Wu has been arrogant for a long time, and his kingdom harbors many treacherous elements.
translations.idiomatic: He was transferred to serve as Chancellor of Wu. When he took his leave to depart, Zhong said to Ang, 'The King of Wu has been arrogant for years, and his kingdom is rife with sedition.

sentences.zh: 今茍欲劾治，彼不上書告君，即利劍刺君矣。

translations.literal: If you now try to impeach and bring order, he will either submit a memorial accusing you, or stab you with a sharp sword.
translations.idiomatic: If you try to investigate and prosecute him, he will either submit a memorial denouncing you, or run you through with a sword.

sentences.zh: 南方卑溼，君能日飲，毋何，時說王曰毋反而已。

translations.literal: The south is low-lying and damp. You should just drink every day, do nothing, and from time to time persuade the king not to rebel—that is all.
translations.idiomatic: The south is low and damp. Just drink every day, do nothing of consequence, and from time to time urge the king not to rebel. That is all you need do.

sentences.zh: 如此幸得脫。」

translations.literal: In this way you may be fortunate enough to escape with your life.'
translations.idiomatic: If you do this, you may be lucky enough to escape with your life.'

sentences.zh: 盎用種之計，吳王厚遇盎。

translations.literal: Ang followed Zhong's plan, and the King of Wu treated Ang generously.
translations.idiomatic: Ang followed Zhong's advice, and the King of Wu treated him well.

sentences.zh: 盎告歸，道逢丞相申屠嘉，下車拜謁，丞相從車上謝袁盎。

translations.literal: When Ang took leave to return home, he encountered Chancellor Shentu Jia on the road. Ang descended from his carriage and made a formal bow. The Chancellor merely returned the greeting from atop his carriage.
translations.idiomatic: When Ang took leave to return home, he met Chancellor Shentu Jia on the road. Ang descended from his carriage and bowed formally, but the Chancellor returned the greeting from his carriage without getting down.

sentences.zh: 袁盎還，愧其吏，乃之丞相舍上謁，求見丞相。

translations.literal: When Yuan Ang returned, feeling humiliated before his subordinates, he went to the Chancellor's residence, presented his calling card, and requested an audience.
translations.idiomatic: When Yuan Ang returned, feeling humiliated in front of his attendants, he went to the Chancellor's residence, presented his card, and requested an audience.

sentences.zh: 丞相良久而見之。

translations.literal: The Chancellor kept him waiting a long time before receiving him.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor kept him waiting a long while before receiving him.

sentences.zh: 盎因跪曰：「願請閒。」

translations.literal: Ang then knelt and said: 'I wish to request a private audience.'
translations.idiomatic: Ang knelt and said, 'I wish to speak with you in private.'

sentences.zh: 丞相曰：「使君所言公事，之曹與長史掾議，吾且奏之；

translations.literal: The Chancellor said: 'If what you wish to say concerns official business, take it to the bureau and discuss it with the Chief Clerk and his assistants. I will then submit it to the throne.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor said, 'If it is an official matter, discuss it with the Chief Clerk and his assistants in the bureau. I will then present it to the throne.

sentences.zh: 即私邪，吾不受私語。」

translations.literal: If it is a private matter, I do not entertain private talk.'
translations.idiomatic: If it is a private matter, I do not entertain private talk.'

sentences.zh: 袁盎即跪說曰：「君為丞相，自度孰與陳平、絳侯？」

translations.literal: Yuan Ang then knelt and said: 'As Chancellor, how do you compare yourself with Chen Ping and the Marquis of Jiang?'
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang knelt and asked, 'As Chancellor, how do you rate yourself against Chen Ping and the Marquis of Jiang?'

sentences.zh: 丞相曰：「吾不如。」

translations.literal: The Chancellor said: 'I am not their equal.'
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor replied, 'I am not their equal.'

sentences.zh: 袁盎曰：「善，君即自謂不如。

translations.literal: Yuan Ang said: 'Good—you yourself admit you are not their equal.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang said, 'Good—you yourself admit you are not their equal.

sentences.zh: 夫陳平、絳侯輔翼高帝，定天下，為將相，而誅諸呂，存劉氏；

translations.literal: Chen Ping and the Marquis of Jiang assisted Emperor Gao, pacified the realm, served as generals and chancellors, and later destroyed the Lü clan to preserve the House of Liu.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping and the Marquis of Jiang served as the wings of Emperor Gao, pacified the realm, served as generals and chancellors, and later destroyed the Lü clan to preserve the House of Liu.

sentences.zh: 君乃為材官蹶張，遷為隊率，積功至淮陽守，非有奇計攻城野戰之功。

translations.literal: But you began as a crossbow soldier, were promoted to squad leader, and accumulated merit until you became Governor of Huaiyang—without any brilliant stratagems, siege victories, or battlefield exploits.
translations.idiomatic: But you began as a crossbow soldier, rose to squad leader, and climbed through accumulated merit to Governor of Huaiyang—without a single brilliant stratagem or battlefield exploit to your name.

sentences.zh: 且陛下從代來，每朝，郎官上書疏，未嘗不止輦受其言，言不可用置之，言可受採之，未嘗不稱善。

translations.literal: Moreover, since His Majesty came from Dai, at every court session, whenever the Gentlemen submit memorials, he invariably stops his carriage to receive their words. If a proposal is not useful, he sets it aside; if it is acceptable, he adopts it, and he never fails to praise it as good.
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, since His Majesty came from Dai, whenever the Gentlemen submit memorials at court, he always stops his carriage to hear their words. Proposals he cannot use he sets aside; those he can accept he adopts, and he never fails to commend them.

sentences.zh: 何也？

translations.literal: Why is this?
translations.idiomatic: And why does he do this?

sentences.zh: 則欲以致天下賢士大夫。

translations.literal: It is because he wishes to attract the worthy scholars and officials of the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Because he wishes to draw the worthy men of the realm to his court.

sentences.zh: 上日聞所不聞，明所不知，日益聖智；

translations.literal: The Emperor daily hears what he had not heard before and understands what he had not known before, growing daily in sagely wisdom.
translations.idiomatic: Each day the Emperor learns what he did not know before, understands what was once obscure, and grows daily in wisdom.

sentences.zh: 君今自閉鉗天下之口而日益愚。

translations.literal: Yet you now shut yourself off and clamp shut the mouths of all under heaven, growing daily in foolishness.
translations.idiomatic: Yet you shut yourself off and clamp shut the mouths of all under heaven, growing more foolish by the day.

sentences.zh: 夫以聖主責愚相，君受禍不久矣。」

translations.literal: When a sagely sovereign holds a foolish chancellor to account, disaster will not be long in coming for you.'
translations.idiomatic: When a wise sovereign calls a foolish chancellor to account, disaster will not be long in coming for you.'

sentences.zh: 丞相乃再拜曰：「嘉鄙野人，乃不知，將軍幸教。」

translations.literal: The Chancellor then bowed twice and said: 'Jia is but a crude and rustic man who did not understand. I am fortunate that the General has instructed me.'
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor bowed twice and said, 'I, Jia, am but a crude and rustic man who did not understand. I am fortunate that the General has deigned to instruct me.'

sentences.zh: 引入與坐，為上客。

translations.literal: He invited Ang in to sit with him, treating him as an honored guest.
translations.idiomatic: He invited Ang in to sit with him and treated him as a guest of honor.

sentences.zh: 盎素不好鼂錯，鼂錯所居坐，盎去；

translations.literal: Ang had always disliked Chao Cuo. Whenever Chao Cuo was present and seated, Ang would leave.
translations.idiomatic: Ang had always disliked Chao Cuo. Whenever Chao Cuo was present, Ang would leave.

sentences.zh: 盎坐，錯亦去：兩人未嘗同堂語。

translations.literal: When Ang was seated, Cuo would likewise leave. The two never exchanged a word in the same hall.
translations.idiomatic: When Ang was present, Cuo would likewise depart. The two never spoke in the same room.

sentences.zh: 及孝文帝崩，孝景帝即位，鼂錯為御史大夫，使吏案袁盎受吳王財物，抵罪，詔赦以為庶人。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xiaowen died and Emperor Xiaojing ascended the throne, Chao Cuo became the Imperial Secretary. He dispatched officials to investigate Yuan Ang for accepting gifts from the King of Wu, and Ang was convicted. By imperial edict he was pardoned but reduced to the status of a commoner.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wen died and Emperor Jing came to the throne, Chao Cuo was appointed Imperial Secretary. He sent officials to investigate Yuan Ang for accepting gifts from the King of Wu, and Ang was convicted. By imperial edict he was pardoned but reduced to a commoner.

sentences.zh: 吳楚反，聞，鼂錯謂丞史曰：「夫袁盎多受吳王金錢，專為蔽匿，言不反。

translations.literal: When Wu and Chu rebelled, upon hearing the news, Chao Cuo said to the Chancellor's Clerk: 'Yuan Ang accepted large sums of gold and money from the King of Wu and deliberately concealed and covered for him, claiming he would not rebel.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu and Chu rebelled, upon hearing the news, Chao Cuo said to the Chancellor's Clerk, 'Yuan Ang accepted large sums from the King of Wu and deliberately covered for him, insisting he would not rebel.

sentences.zh: 今果反，欲請治盎宜知計謀。」

translations.literal: Now he has indeed rebelled. I wish to request an investigation of Ang, who must have known of the plot.'
translations.idiomatic: Now that the rebellion has come, I intend to request an investigation of Ang, who surely knew of the plot.'

sentences.zh: 丞史曰：「事未發，治之有絕。

translations.literal: The Chancellor's Clerk said: 'Before the affair broke out, pursuing it might have put an end to it.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor's Clerk replied, 'Before the rebellion broke out, an investigation might have nipped it in the bud.

sentences.zh: 今兵西鄉，治之何益！

translations.literal: Now that the armies are marching westward, what good would an investigation do!
translations.idiomatic: Now that their armies are marching west, what good would an investigation do!

sentences.zh: 且袁盎不宜有謀。」

translations.literal: Moreover, Yuan Ang is unlikely to have been party to the plot.'
translations.idiomatic: Besides, it is unlikely that Yuan Ang was party to the conspiracy.'

sentences.zh: 鼂錯猶與未決。

translations.literal: Chao Cuo still hesitated and had not yet decided.
translations.idiomatic: Chao Cuo hesitated and could not make up his mind.

sentences.zh: 人有告袁盎者，袁盎恐，夜見竇嬰，為言吳所以反者，願至上前口對狀。

translations.literal: Someone informed Yuan Ang of this. Ang was alarmed and went to see Dou Ying by night. He explained the reasons for Wu's rebellion and expressed his wish to present an oral defense before the Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Someone warned Yuan Ang. Alarmed, Ang went to see Dou Ying that night. He explained the reasons for Wu's rebellion and expressed his wish to present his case in person before the Emperor.

sentences.zh: 竇嬰入言上，上乃召袁盎入見。

translations.literal: Dou Ying went in and spoke to the Emperor, and the Emperor thereupon summoned Yuan Ang for an audience.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Ying went in and spoke to the Emperor, who summoned Yuan Ang for an audience.

sentences.zh: 鼂錯在前，及盎請辟人賜閒，錯去，固恨甚。

translations.literal: Chao Cuo was present at the time. When Ang requested that others be dismissed for a private audience, Cuo was forced to withdraw, and his resentment deepened greatly.
translations.idiomatic: Chao Cuo was present at the audience. When Ang asked that others be dismissed for a private word, Cuo had to leave, and his resentment deepened.

sentences.zh: 袁盎具言吳所以反狀，以錯故，獨急斬錯以謝吳，吳兵乃可罷。

translations.literal: Yuan Ang laid out in full the reasons for Wu's rebellion, arguing that it was on account of Cuo, and that the only course was to swiftly execute Cuo as an apology to Wu, after which the Wu armies could be halted.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang laid out in full the reasons for Wu's rebellion, arguing that Cuo was the cause, and that the only way to halt the Wu armies was to execute Cuo at once as a gesture of appeasement to Wu.

sentences.zh: 其語具在吳事中。

translations.literal: The full account of his words is in the chapter on the affairs of Wu.
translations.idiomatic: The full account of his words can be found in the chapter on the affairs of Wu.

sentences.zh: 使袁盎為太常，竇嬰為大將軍。

translations.literal: Yuan Ang was appointed Grand Master of Ceremonies, and Dou Ying was made Grand General.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang was appointed Grand Master of Ceremonies, and Dou Ying was made Grand General.

sentences.zh: 兩人素相與善。

translations.literal: The two had always been on good terms with each other.
translations.idiomatic: The two had always been close.

sentences.zh: 逮吳反。

translations.literal: This was at the time of the Wu rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: This was at the time of the Wu rebellion.

sentences.zh: 諸陵長者長安中賢大夫爭附兩人，車隨者日數百乘。

translations.literal: The elders of the various imperial mausoleum towns and the worthy officials of Chang'an all vied to attach themselves to the two men. Hundreds of carriages followed them each day.
translations.idiomatic: The elders of the mausoleum towns and the eminent officials of Chang'an vied to attach themselves to the two men, and hundreds of carriages trailed them each day.

sentences.zh: 及鼂錯已誅，袁盎以太常使吳。

translations.literal: After Chao Cuo had been executed, Yuan Ang was sent as Grand Master of Ceremonies on a mission to Wu.
translations.idiomatic: After Chao Cuo was executed, Yuan Ang was dispatched as Grand Master of Ceremonies on a mission to Wu.

sentences.zh: 吳王欲使將，不肯。

translations.literal: The King of Wu wished to make him a general, but he refused.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Wu wanted to make him a general, but Ang refused.

sentences.zh: 欲殺之，使一都尉以五百人圍守盎軍中。

translations.literal: The king wished to kill him, and dispatched a commandant with five hundred men to surround and guard Ang in the military camp.
translations.idiomatic: The king wanted to kill him and sent a commandant with five hundred soldiers to surround and guard Ang in the camp.

sentences.zh: 袁盎自其為吳相時，（嘗）有從史嘗盜愛盎侍兒，盎知之，弗泄，遇之如故。

translations.literal: When Yuan Ang had served as Chancellor of Wu, one of his attendant clerks had secretly taken a maidservant whom Ang favored. Ang knew of it but did not expose the matter, treating the clerk just as before.
translations.idiomatic: When Yuan Ang had served as Chancellor of Wu, one of his attendant clerks had secretly carried on with a maidservant Ang favored. Ang knew of it but said nothing, treating the man just as before.

sentences.zh: 人有告從史，言「君知爾與侍者通」，乃亡歸。

translations.literal: Someone told the attendant clerk: 'Your lord knows that you have been carrying on with his maid.' The clerk thereupon fled and returned home.
translations.idiomatic: Someone told the clerk, 'Your lord knows you have been carrying on with his maid.' The clerk fled and went home.

sentences.zh: 袁盎驅自追之，遂以侍者賜之，復為從史。
及袁盎使吳見守，從史適為守盎校尉司馬，乃悉以其裝齎置二石醇醪，會天寒，士卒饑渴，飲酒醉，西南陬卒皆臥，司馬夜引袁盎起，曰：「君可以去矣，吳王期旦日斬君。」
盎弗信，曰：「公何為者？」
司馬曰：「臣故為從史盜君侍兒者。」
盎乃驚謝曰；
「公幸有親，吾不足以累公。」
司馬曰：「君弟去，臣亦且亡，辟吾親，君何患！」
乃以刀決張，道從醉卒（直）隧［直］出。
司馬與分背，袁盎解節毛懷之，杖，步行七八里，明，見梁騎，騎馳去，遂歸報。
吳楚已破，上更以元王子平陸侯禮為楚王，袁盎為楚相。
嘗上書有所言，不用。
袁盎病免居家，與閭里浮沈，相隨行，鬬雞走狗。
雒陽劇孟嘗過袁盎，盎善待之。
安陵富人有謂盎曰：「吾聞劇孟博徒，將軍何自通之？」
盎曰：「劇孟雖博徒，然母死，客送葬車千餘乘，此亦有過人者。
且緩急人所有。
夫一旦有急叩門，不以親為解，不以存亡為辭，天下所望者，獨季心、劇孟耳。
今公常從數騎，一旦有緩急，寧足恃乎！」
罵富人，弗與通。
諸公聞之，皆多袁盎。
袁盎雖家居，景帝時時使人問籌策。
梁王欲求為嗣，袁盎進說，其後語塞。
梁王以此怨盎，曾使人刺盎。
刺者至關中，問袁盎，諸君譽之皆不容口。
乃見袁盎曰：「臣受梁王金來刺君，君長者，不忍刺君。
然後刺君者十餘曹，備之！」
袁盎心不樂，家又多怪，乃之棓生所問占。
還，梁刺客後曹輩果遮刺殺盎安陵郭門外。
鼂錯者，潁川人也。
學申商刑名於軹張恢先所，與雒陽宋孟及劉禮同師。
以文學為太常掌故。
錯為人陗直刻深。
孝文帝時，天下無治尚書者，獨聞濟南伏生故秦博士，治尚書，年九十餘，老不可徵，乃詔太常使人往受之。
太常遣錯受尚書伏生所。
還，因上便宜事，以書稱說。
詔以為太子舍人、門大夫、家令。
以其辯得幸太子，太子家號曰「智囊」。
數上書孝文時，言削諸侯事，及法令可更定者。
書數十上，孝文不聽，然奇其材，遷為中大夫。
當是時，太子善錯計策，袁盎諸大功臣多不好錯。
景帝即位，以錯為內史。
錯常數請閒言事，輒聽，寵幸傾九卿，法令多所更定。
丞相申屠嘉心弗便，力未有以傷。
內史府居太上廟壖中，門東出，不便，錯乃穿兩門南出，鑿廟壖垣。
丞相嘉聞，大怒，欲因此過為奏請誅錯。
錯聞之，即夜請閒，具為上言之。
丞相奏事，因言錯擅鑿廟垣為門，請下廷尉誅。
上曰：「此非廟垣，乃壖中垣，不致於法。」
丞相謝。
罷朝，怒謂長史曰：「吾當先斬以聞，乃先請，為兒所賣，固誤。」
丞相遂發病死。
錯以此愈貴。
遷為御史大夫，請諸侯之罪過，削其地，收其枝郡。
奏上，上令公卿列侯宗室集議，莫敢難，獨竇嬰爭之，由此與錯有卻。
錯所更令三十章，諸侯皆諠譁疾鼂錯。
錯父聞之，從潁川來，謂錯曰：「上初即位，公為政用事，侵削諸侯，別疏人骨肉，人口議多怨公者，何也？」
鼂錯曰：「固也。
不如此，天子不尊，宗廟不安。」
錯父曰：「劉氏安矣，而鼂氏危矣，吾去公歸矣！」
遂飲藥死，曰：「吾不忍見禍及吾身。」
死十餘日，吳楚七國果反，以誅錯為名。
及竇嬰、袁盎進說，上令鼂錯衣朝衣斬東市。
鼂錯已死，謁者仆射鄧公為校尉，擊吳楚軍為將。
還，上書言軍事，謁見上。
上問曰：「道軍所來，聞鼂錯死，吳楚罷不？」
鄧公曰：「吳王為反數十年矣，發怒削地，以誅錯為名，其意非在錯也。
且臣恐天下之士噤口，不敢復言也！」
上曰：「何哉？」
鄧公曰：「夫鼂錯患諸侯彊大不可制，故請削地以尊京師，萬世之利也。
計畫始行，卒受大戮，內杜忠臣之口，外為諸侯報仇，臣竊為陛下不取也。」
於是景帝默然良久，曰：「公言善，吾亦恨之。」
乃拜鄧公為城陽中尉。
鄧公，成固人也，多奇計。
建元中，上招賢良，公卿言鄧公，時鄧公免，起家為九卿。
一年，復謝病免歸。
其子章以修黃老言顯於諸公閒。
太史公曰：袁盎雖不好學，亦善傅會，仁心為質，引義慨。
遭孝文初立，資適逢世。
時以變易，及吳楚一說，說雖行哉，然復不遂。
好聲矜賢，竟以名敗。
鼂錯為家令時，數言事不用；
後擅權，多所變更。
諸侯發難，不急匡救，欲報私讎，反以亡軀。
語曰「變古亂常，不死則亡」，豈錯等謂邪！
